behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .alexignore, the required header '# Next.js Development Guide', sections (Git Workflow, Build Commands, Testing, Linting and Types), the >5000 char threshold, and the symlink target.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes desired end state (AGENTS.md as regular file, CLAUDE.md as symlink, .alexignore lists both) without dictating exact commands.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for symlink target == 'AGENTS.md', file content matching, and presence of strings — any correct implementation satisfying these will pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests use Path.is_symlink, os.readlink, and subprocess cat to verify actual filesystem behavior, not just text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests require AGENTS.md to be a real file with >5000 chars, CLAUDE.md to be a symlink to AGENTS.md, and .alexignore to contain both names — a stub cannot pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All required behaviors (AGENTS.md content, CLAUDE.md symlink, .alexignore entries, section headers, size) are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (pass_to_pass vs fail_to_pass) and have descriptive names and docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data.

no_typos.reason: Filenames AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .alexignore are consistent throughout instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the obvious files (CLAUDE.md, .alexignore) which are the task domain itself; no extraneous line-level hints.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The .alexignore edit is a small additive change (adding AGENTS.md line); not a full-file rewrite.
